title: 烏大龜請入黎，有條物理題想請教下你

replies.msg: A circular thin plate of radius R and weight W is floating on the water which has a density of p as shown. The plate is in equilibrium. Find the weight W in terms of p,R and r. Assume that atmospheric pressure is negligible.

You should find the volume of the immersed part first.

Recall Mass = density x volume
and Force = Mass x acceleration due to density
Also Potential Energy = Mass x Gravity x Distance

You should find the volume of the immersed part first.

Recall Mass = density x volume
and Force = Mass x acceleration due to gravity
Also Potential Energy = Mass x Gravity x Distance


Definite Integration is required.

Show your steps please.

The thickness of the plate is unknown.
Poor question-setting.

Assume that the plate is very thin so the thickness is negligible.

The thickness of the plate is unknown.
Poor question-setting.

Assume that the plate is very thin so the thickness is negligible.

So Area of immersed part = Volume of immersed part?

Yes. We are dealing with area in this case. There is no volume.
